name: Macarons
instructions: 1. Place egg whites into a metal mixing bowl and refrigerate overnight. 2. The next day, bring egg whites to room temperature. Preheat oven to 280 degrees Fahrenheit (138 degrees Celsius). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. 3. Whisk the confectioners' sugar and almond flour together in a bowl. 4. Beat egg whites with a pinch of salt in a metal bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed until foamy, about 1 minute. Gradually increase speed to high, adding superfine sugar at a time. Beat until egg whites are glossy and hold stiff peaks, 3 to 5 minutes. 5. Gently fold the almond flour mixture into the egg whites until thoroughly incorporated. Spoon mixture into a pastry bag with a 3/small tip. 6. Pipe small disks onto prepared baking sheet, leaving a few inches apart. Let stand at room temperature until shiny surfaces become dull and a thin skin forms, about 25 to 30 minutes. 7. Bake in preheated oven for 19 to 20 minutes. Let cool on baking sheet before removing. 8. Spread half of the macarons with desired filling, top with remaining macarons to make sandwiches. Refrigerate at least 2 hours or overnight before serving.
simpleInstructions: 1. Refrigerate egg whites overnight and bring to room temperature the next day. 2. Preheat oven to 280 degrees Fahrenheit, line baking sheet. 3. Mix the almond flour and confectioners' sugar. 4. Beat egg whites and sugar until stiff peaks form. 5. Fold the flour mixture into egg whites, pipe onto sheet, let stand, then bake. 6. Cool, fill, and refrigerate.
